records.ety: [Scot. glamour, glamer; cf. Icel. glmeggdr one who is troubled with the glaucoma (?); or Icel. glām-sni weakness of sight, glamour; glāmr name of the moon, also of a ghost + sni sight, akin to E. see. Perh., however, a corruption of E. gramarye.]

defs: A charm affecting the eye, making objects appear different from what they really are.
A kind of haze in the air, causing things to appear different from what they really are.
quotes: The air filled with a strange, pale glamour that seemed to lie over the broad valley.

defs: Any artificial interest in, or association with, an object, through which it appears delusively magnified or glorified.
quotes: It had much of glamour might To make a lady seem a knight.
